hold_window_rule_context: Both active_loop_prompt_repair and post_hold_reentry_contract_repair already exist in this window. This is a concrete runtime/code repair that improves the odds of the scheduled post-hold lane succeeding.
summary: content_gap_analysis() in seo_daily.py only scanned the homepage for keyword coverage, returning a misleading 12.5% when all 25 blog posts on ralphworkflow.com actually cover all 8 priority keywords. Fixed to scan the full sitemap (blog posts first), producing correct 100% coverage.

before.downstream_effect: Every SEO report recommended 'create content targeting X' for gaps that were already covered by published blog posts — wasted engineering cycles

after.scan_method: homepage + sitemap (blog URLs prioritized, up to 50 fetches)
after.priority_action_shift: was 'create content targeting gaps' → now 'build backlinks' (correct: content created, need distribution)

seo_daily.py: Added _check_url_for_keywords() — fetches individual URLs for keyword presence
Rewrote content_gap_analysis() — homepage + sitemap scan with blog-prioritized ordering
Cap 50 URL fetches to stay under ~10s runtime budget

test: python3 -c 'from agents.marketing.seo_daily import content_gap_analysis, PRIORITY_KEYWORDS, COMPETITORS; r = content_gap_analysis(PRIORITY_KEYWORDS, COMPETITORS); assert r["coverage_pct"] == 100.0'
